channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Muslims begin the annual Hajj against a backdrop of war concerns
item.description: More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions.
item.content:encoded:
More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions.

(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China launches Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 1 of 3 astronauts set for yearlong stay
item.description: China launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 3 astronauts heading to its space station. One astronaut is set to stay in space for a year to explore human adaptability in long-duration spaceflights.
item.content:encoded:
China launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 3 astronauts heading to its space station. One astronaut is set to stay in space for a year to explore human adaptability in long-duration spaceflights.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says a deal to end war with Iran is near, but many questions remain
item.description: U.S. and Iran are nearing a tentative deal to end the conflict, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and curb Tehran's uranium stockpile — though major details remain unresolved.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and Iran are nearing a tentative deal to end the conflict, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and curb Tehran's uranium stockpile — though major details remain unresolved.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Has the U.S. lost the war in Iran?
item.description: As both the US and Iran signal a peace deal is near, Robert Kagan, a Senior Fellow at the Brookings Institution, says the U.S. will likely come out weaker than before the war.
item.content:encoded: As both the US and Iran signal a peace deal is near, Robert Kagan, a Senior Fellow at the Brookings Institution, says the U.S. will likely come out weaker than before the war.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Remains of lost arctic explorers identified with DNA, nearly two centuries later
item.description: New research shines a light on the lost Franklin Expedition, a 19th century voyage to the Canadian Arctic gone awry.
item.content:encoded: New research shines a light on the lost Franklin Expedition, a 19th century voyage to the Canadian Arctic gone awry.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: DR Congo Ebola cases rise amid distrust, armed conflict zone
item.description: Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak threatening 10 countries as infections spill from eastern Congo into Uganda.
item.content:encoded:
Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak threatening 10 countries as infections spill from eastern Congo into Uganda.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: What both sides have said about a U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: President Trump took to social media Saturday and said the U.S. and Iran are close to deal on ending the war. But the president didn't offer details and it's not yet clear where Iran stands.
item.content:encoded: President Trump took to social media Saturday and said the U.S. and Iran are close to deal on ending the war. But the president didn't offer details and it's not yet clear where Iran stands.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: What we know about the status of a U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Ariane Tabatabaithe, Public Service Fellow at Lawfare, about where things stand on a potential deal between the U.S. and Iran on ending the war.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Ariane Tabatabaithe, Public Service Fellow at Lawfare, about where things stand on a potential deal between the U.S. and Iran on ending the war.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Russia pounds Kyiv in powerful drone and missile attack
item.description: At least two people were killed and 77 injured in the attack, which included the use of a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile called the Oreshnik, which is capable of carrying a nuclear warhead.
item.content:encoded:
At least two people were killed and 77 injured in the attack, which included the use of a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile called the Oreshnik, which is capable of carrying a nuclear warhead.

(Image credit: Evgeniy Maloletka)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu's 'Fjord' wins top prize at Cannes
item.description: Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.
item.content:encoded:
Fjord — and Sebastian Stan, during the awards ceremony at the 79th Cannes international film festival, in southern France, on Saturday.'/>

Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.

(Image credit: Andreea Alexandru)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster
